entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A multi-purpose, versatile food legume (Cajanus cajan) known for its drought tolerance and ability to withstand harsh environments

entities.name: PLANT ARCHITECTURE
entities.description: The physical structure and arrangement of the pigeonpea plant

entities.name: DURATION
entities.description: The time period from planting to maturity in pigeonpea

entities.name: YIELD PATTERN
entities.description: The distribution and timing of yield production in pigeonpea

entities.name: RAINFED ECOLOGIES
entities.description: Agricultural environments where water is supplied solely by rainfall

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A condition of water scarcity that causes losses in various crops

entities.name: CEREALS
entities.description: A group of crops including grasses cultivated for edible grains

entities.name: MILLETS
entities.description: A group of small-seeded grasses used as food crops

entities.name: OIL SEEDS
entities.description: Crops grown specifically for the extraction of oil from their seeds

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to maintain growth and productivity during water-deficient periods

entities.name: HARSH ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Extreme environmental conditions that challenge crop survival and growth

entities.name: SUPER EARLY MATURITY
entities.description: A trait where the crop reaches maturity in less than 100 days

entities.name: MATURITY DURATION
entities.description: The total time taken by a plant to reach maturity, categorized as early or medium

entities.name: EARLINESS
entities.description: The trait of maturing quickly, which helps in escaping drought stress

entities.name: PHOTO INSENSITIVITY
entities.description: The ability of a plant to flower independently of the day length

entities.name: PER DAY PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of yield produced by the plant on a daily basis

entities.name: ADAPTABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of a genotype to perform well across varying altitudes and environments

entities.name: ALTITUDES
entities.description: The vertical distance above sea level which affects crop growth

entities.name: STRESS ESCAPE MECHANISM
entities.description: A biological strategy to avoid environmental stress by completing the life cycle before the stress occurs

entities.name: CROPPING SYSTEMS
entities.description: The specific arrangement and sequence of crops grown on a piece of land

entities.name: RAPID GENERATION TURNOVER
entities.description: A breeding method used to accelerate the production of multiple generations per year

entities.name: INTROGRESSION
entities.description: The process of incorporating specific traits from one genetic background into another

entities.name: BIOTIC AND ABIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Stress factors caused by living organisms (biotic) or non-living environmental factors (abiotic)

entities.name: MAPPING POPULATION
entities.description: A set of individuals used in genetic studies to identify the location of traits or genes

entities.name: SUPER EARLY VARIETIES AND HYBRIDS
entities.description: Breeding materials characterized by super early maturity, used to secure sustainable dry land production

entities.name: DRY LAND PRODUCTION
entities.description: Agricultural production in regions characterized by low rainfall and water scarcity
